items.title: PyReveal
items.text: PyReveal is a Python library for building Reveal.js presentations programmatically. It bundles reveal.js 6.x and writes self-contained HTML decks you can open locally or host anywhere.

items.title: Why PyReveal?
items.text:
• Object-oriented API — compose decks with Slide, Element, and Style
  • Reveal.js 6 — themes, transitions, scroll view, and more via configure()
  • Standalone output — save_to_file() copies reveal.js assets into the output folder

items.title: Quick example
items.text:
from pyreveal import PyReveal, Slide

presentation = PyReveal(title="Hello", theme="white", transition="fade")
presentation.configure(hash=True, progress=True)

intro = Slide(content="<h1>Welcome</h1>")
intro.add_vertical_slide(Slide(content="<p>Details</p>"))
presentation.add_slide(intro)

presentation.save_to_file("deck.html")

items.title: Documentation versions
items.text: This site is versioned with mike and Zensical. Use the version selector in the header to switch between releases.

items.title: Next steps
items.text:
• Installation
  • Quick start
  • Slides

items.title: Development setup
items.text:
git clone https://github.com/tavallaie/pyreveal.git
cd pyreveal
git submodule update --init --recursive
uv sync --dev --group docs

items.title: Run tests
items.text: uv run pytest

items.title: Build the package
items.text: uv build

items.title: Documentation
items.text:
Preview docs locally:

uv run zensical serve

Build a static site:

uv run zensical build --clean

See Documentation versioning for publishing versioned docs to GitHub Pages.

items.title: Documentation versioning
items.text: PyReveal documentation uses Zensical with mike for versioned deployments on GitHub Pages, following the Zensical versioning guide.

items.title: How it works
items.text:
• Each release is published under https://tavallaie.github.io/pyreveal/<version>/
  • The latest alias points to the current release
  • The site root redirects to latest
  • A version selector appears in the documentation header

Configuration lives in zensical.toml:

[project.extra.version]
provider = "mike"

items.title: Local preview
items.text:
Serve the current working tree (single version):

uv sync --group docs
uv run zensical serve

Preview all deployed versions after a local mike deploy:

uv run mike serve

items.title: Publish a new version manually
items.text:
Use the major.minor version (for example 0.4 for package version 0.4.0):

uv sync --group docs
git fetch origin gh-pages --depth=1 || true

uv run mike deploy --push --update-aliases 0.4 latest
uv run mike set-default --push latest

items.title: CI deployment
items.text:
Pushes to main and dev run .github/workflows/docs.yml, which:

  1. Reads the package version from pyproject.toml
  2. Deploys docs with mike deploy
  3. Updates the latest alias and default redirect

items.title: First-time setup
items.text:
If the gh-pages branch does not exist yet, the first workflow run creates it. Enable GitHub Pages for the repository:

Settings → Pages → Deploy from branch → gh-pages → / (root)

items.title: Requirements
items.text:
• Python 3.10 or newer
  • uv (recommended) or pip

items.title: Install from PyPI
items.text:
pip install pyreveal

Or with uv:

uv add pyreveal

items.title: Install from source
items.text:
Clone the repository and initialize the reveal.js submodule:

git clone https://github.com/tavallaie/pyreveal.git
cd pyreveal
git submodule update --init --recursive
uv sync --dev

reveal.js 6.x ships pre-built dist/ files in the submodule, so you do not need Node.js to use PyReveal.

items.title: Verify the install
items.text: uv run python -c "from pyreveal import PyReveal; print(PyReveal.__module__)"

items.title: Quick start
items.text:
This guide uses the canonical Slide-based API. See Slides for vertical stacks, groups, and backgrounds.

from pyreveal import PyReveal, Slide

presentation = PyReveal(
    title="My Presentation",
    theme="black",
    transition="slide",
)

presentation.configure(hash=True, progress=True, slideNumber="c/t")

intro = Slide(content="<h1>Welcome to PyReveal</h1>")
intro.add_vertical_slide(Slide(content="<p>First vertical slide</p>"))
intro.add_vertical_slide(Slide(content="<p>Second vertical slide</p>"))
presentation.add_slide(intro)

presentation.add_slide(Slide(content="<h2>Another horizontal slide</h2>"))

presentation.save_to_file("my_presentation.html")

Open presentations/my_presentation.html in a browser. The presentations/ folder also contains copied revealjs/ assets.

items.title: Examples in the repository
items.text:
• example/basic_usage.py — slides and vertical stacks
  • example/elements_usage.py — ImageElement, Style, and add_group()

items.title: Backgrounds
items.text: Slide backgrounds map to reveal.js data-background-* attributes.

items.title: Color
items.text:
from pyreveal import ColorBackground, Slide

slide = Slide(
    content="<h2>Colored slide</h2>",
    background=ColorBackground("#2d3436"),
)

items.title: Image
items.text:
from pyreveal import ImageBackground, Slide

slide = Slide(
    content="<h2>Photo background</h2>",
    background=ImageBackground("photos/bg.jpg", size="cover"),
)

items.title: Video
items.text:
from pyreveal import Slide, VideoBackground

slide = Slide(
    content="<h2>Video background</h2>",
    background=VideoBackground("assets/loop.mp4"),
)

items.title: Factory helper
items.text:
from pyreveal import BackgroundFactory

bg = BackgroundFactory.create_background("color", "#1a1a2e")
bg = BackgroundFactory.create_background("image", "bg.jpg", size="cover")
bg = BackgroundFactory.create_background("video", "loop.mp4")

items.title: Reveal.js configuration
items.text:
Use configure() to pass options to Reveal.initialize() in the generated HTML. See the reveal.js config reference for all keys.

presentation = PyReveal(theme="white", transition="slide")
presentation.configure(
    hash=True,
    progress=True,
    slideNumber="h.v",
    controls=True,
    view="scroll",
)

items.title: Theme vs transition
items.text: Setting How to set Notes Theme set_theme("dracula") Loads revealjs/dist/theme/<name>.css Transition set_transition("fade") or configure(transition="fade") configure() wins at render time

items.title: Supported themes
items.text: beige, black, black-contrast, blood, dracula, league, moon, night, serif, simple, sky, solarized, white, white-contrast

items.title: Supported transitions
items.text: none, slide, fade, convex, concave, zoom

items.title: Output directory
items.text: presentation.save_to_file("deck.html", output_dir="build/my-talk")

items.title: Elements and styles
items.text: Slides can include structured HTML via Element subclasses and optional Style objects.

items.title: Image elements
items.text:
from pyreveal import ImageElement, PyReveal, Slide, Style

slide = Slide(content="<h2>Chart</h2>")
slide.add_element(
    ImageElement(
        image_url="assets/chart.png",
        alt_text="Sales chart",
        style=Style(width="480px", margin="0 auto"),
    )
)
presentation.add_slide(slide)

When you call save_to_file(), image paths referenced by elements are copied into the output assets/ directory.

items.title: Video elements
items.text:
from pyreveal import Slide, VideoElement

slide = Slide(content="<h2>Demo</h2>")
slide.add_element(VideoElement(video_url="assets/demo.mp4"))

items.title: Generic elements
items.text: Element supports arbitrary tags, attributes, nested children, and inline CSS via Style.

items.title: Slides
items.text: A Slide holds HTML content, an optional background, child elements, and optional vertical slides.

items.title: Basic slide
items.text:
from pyreveal import PyReveal, Slide

presentation = PyReveal()
presentation.add_slide(Slide(content="<h1>Title</h1><p>Body</p>"))

items.title: Vertical slides
items.text:
Nest slides under a horizontal slide with add_vertical_slide():

parent = Slide(content="<h1>Section</h1>")
parent.add_vertical_slide(Slide(content="<p>Point A</p>"))
parent.add_vertical_slide(Slide(content="<p>Point B</p>"))
presentation.add_slide(parent)

items.title: Vertical groups
items.text:
Use add_group() when you want a vertical stack without a separate parent slide object:

presentation.add_group([
    Slide(content="<h2>Agenda</h2>"),
    Slide(content="<p>Topic 1</p>"),
    Slide(content="<p>Topic 2</p>"),
])

items.title: Deprecated string API
items.text:
add_content_slide(content=...) still works but emits a DeprecationWarning. Migrate to Slide objects:

# deprecated
presentation.add_content_slide("<p>Hello</p>")

# preferred
presentation.add_slide(Slide(content="<p>Hello</p>"))
